LanguageData.VoidMonolith.label: Big MT antenna
LanguageData.VoidMonolith.description: A pre-war broadcast antenna of Big MT construction. Its control panel bears the stylized atom logo and dense scientific notation of the Big MT Research and Development corporation. The antenna hums with low-frequency static, and the signal it's broadcasting has started drawing in something from the region.\n\nThe longer it broadcasts, the stronger the signal grows â€” and the worse whatever's listening gets.
LanguageData.Plant_TreeHarbinger.label: spore plant
LanguageData.ChoppedStump_Harbinger.label: chopped spore plant stump
LanguageData.SmashedStump_Harbinger.label: smashed spore plant stump
LanguageData.HarbingerSeed.label: spore plant seed
LanguageData.Bioferrite.label: Saturnite alloy
LanguageData.Bioferrite.description: A heat-resistant metallic compound developed by Big MT's materials division. Saturnite alloy is extracted from Big MT experiment subjects and byproducts. It has exceptional thermal and structural properties, and can be shaped into a range of weapons and tools under high heat.
LanguageData.GoldenCube.label: toaster
LanguageData.GoldenCube.description:
A pre-war smart appliance from Big MT â€” specifically, the Toaster. Its chassis has been heavily modified at some point in the past, and it now hosts a rudimentary AI personality that considers itself imprisoned. It broadcasts low-level psychic suggestions on a subliminal frequency to anyone nearby, instilling a vague but persistent sense that it would really like to be freed.

It cannot be freed. It is a toaster.

Long-term exposure leads to obsessive fixation on the object. Colonists who spend too long near it begin spending their free time staring at it, talking to it, and eventually refusing to let others near it.
LanguageData.WarpedObelisk_Mutator.label: West Tek mutagenic emitter
LanguageData.PitGate.label: service tunnel entrance
LanguageData.FleshmassHeart.label: FEV reactor
LanguageData.Noctolith.label: FEV containment pod
LanguageData.Gorehulk.label: behemoth
LanguageData.Revenant.label: nightkin
LanguageData.Revenant.description: A super mutant fused with a Big MT Stealth Boy prototype during an experiment gone wrong. The Stealth Boy's electronics have permanently bonded to the mutant's nervous system, bending light around it continuously. Nightkin are rarely visible until they strike, and their long-term Stealth Boy addiction has fractured their psyche â€” they experience hallucinations, phantom pains, and murderous fixations. Capturing one for study is extremely dangerous, and even contained specimens occasionally slip partway into stealth.\n\nUnlike most Big MT entities, nightkin appear to be aware of their condition.
LanguageData.Metalhorror.label: trauma harness
LanguageData.Metalhorror.description:
A Y-17 Trauma Override Harness â€” a pre-war medical exosuit designed to keep injured soldiers moving even after sustaining lethal wounds. The harness's override system has long since outlasted its wearer, and it now operates autonomously, compelled to "keep the soldier moving" by its original trauma-response loop.

The harness seeks out new hosts. It strips the nervous system from incapacitated victims and integrates their body into its override network, adding them to the collective of walking dead it maintains. The harnessed body continues fighting until the harness's power finally fails.

The harness itself is hidden inside the host's torso and can only be detected post-mortem.
LanguageData.Sightstealer.label: roboscorpion
LanguageData.Noctol.label: nightstalker
LanguageData.Noctol.description: A predator bred by Big MT from coyote and rattlesnake genetic material via FEV crossgrafting. Nightstalkers retain the intelligence and pack behavior of the coyote alongside the rattlesnake's venom delivery and subterranean instincts. They are fast, work in coordinated packs, and can deliver venomous bites. Early Big MT reports described them as "a complete success" immediately before the containment break.
LanguageData.Devourer.label: centaur
LanguageData.Devourer.description: An FEV-fused multi-species amalgam â€” human, dog, and other material merged by Forced Evolutionary Virus into a lurching horror with too many limbs and a body plan that ignores anatomical logic. Centaurs were a recurring accident of early FEV batch testing: the virus, when introduced to a mixed-species environment, sometimes fuses subjects rather than transforming them individually. Big MT kept them. They swallow victims whole, digesting them slowly in an internal sac while the victim is still alive.
LanguageData.Chimera.label: FEV hybrid
LanguageData.Nociosphere.label: Think Tank brain
LanguageData.Nociosphere.description: A rogue Think Tank â€” a pre-war scientist's brain, surgically removed and housed inside a life-support sphere that has long since separated from its intended chassis. The brain inside has had considerable time to go insane. It broadcasts psychic pain on a continuous frequency, seemingly in an attempt to communicate. Or as a weapon. It's not entirely clear that it knows the difference anymore.\n\nIf captured and suppressed, the sphere can be studied. The researcher will report breakthrough-level insights, followed by several weeks of nightmares.
LanguageData.FleshmassNucleus.label: ZAX unit
LanguageData.FleshmassNucleus.description: A ZAX supercomputer core that has become fused with Big MT's FEV mass containment vat. The ZAX unit continues to run its original research directives, directing FEV beast growth and coordinating experimental subjects. It is simultaneously the brain and the tumor of the entire FEV mass organism. Destroying it will collapse the FEV mass structure.
LanguageData.Bulbfreak.label: FEV hulk
LanguageData.Bulbfreak.description: A massive amalgamation of FEV-mutated flesh, all connected by a single distributed nervous system. Two enormous clawed limbs tear through anything in reach. FEV hulks are relatively slow but extremely durable, and the viral mass that composes them is unstable â€” when they die, the nervous system distributes command to smaller autonomous sub-units, which burst from the collapsing body as independent FEV beasts.\n\nThe smell of rancid bioma typically announces them before they're seen.
LanguageData.Toughspike.label: FEV soldier
LanguageData.Toughspike.description: A FEV beast engineered â€” perhaps intentionally, perhaps by evolutionary drift â€” for anti-personnel capability. Two hardened keratin-spike limbs launch dense projectiles at range with surprising accuracy. An armored carapace provides the body with substantial protection against conventional arms.\n\nFEV soldier corpses degrade quickly into FEV slurry if not processed immediately.
LanguageData.Trispike.label: FEV spiker
LanguageData.Fingerspike.label: FEV crawler
LanguageData.Dreadmeld.label: FEV amalgam
